data.content: The problem is clear. The target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't know about it. The fix is to include the generated file in compilation:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The MSBuild `Compile` items are collected before `CoreCompile`, so the generated file exists on disk but isn't included in the compilation.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` within the target that includes the generated file in the `Compile` items:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```


This needs to go inside the `GenerateSampleCode` target right after the file is written to disk.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` to the intermediate output directory but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. The compiler doesn't know the file exists.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="...">` item inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

The project now builds and runs successfully.
